content: Terminal recording tools: VHS (charmbracelet) — declarative .tape script, outputs GIF/MP4/WebM/ASCII, NO SVG support, best for CI/GitHub Actions via vhs-action, requires ttyd or a running terminal emulator, produces pixel-perfect GIF animations. Asciinema — records real sessions, requires external player embed (doesn't work inline in GitHub README as script tags are stripped). svg-term-cli — converts asciinema recordings to animated SVG files, renders inline in GitHub README. gen_screenshots.py (existing) — renders via Playwright+Docker, outputs static PNG strips and hand-rolled animated SVG (no external deps for SVG path). For claudebar: the existing animated SVG approach is superior to VHS because it requires no Docker/ttyd in CI and renders inline on GitHub; VHS would be an alternative for a purely GIF-based demo.
